uses: Xanthoselinum alsaticum, also known as meadow rue or alpine meadow rue, is a plant with a history of medicinal use. intro: Xanthoselinum alsaticum, also known as Wild Lovage or Lovage, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Lovage include volatile oils, alkaloids, and glycosides, particularly sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lovage is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used as a flavoring agent in soups, stews, and sauces due to its sweet, celery-like flavor.
